steps.title: Use the constitutional counts
steps.content: The notes say all fifty state constitutions confer the right to vote, but only thirty state constitutions include some form of free-elections clause. The generalization must capture that uneven coverage.

steps.title: Keep the focus on clauses
steps.content: B contradicts the notes by saying every state has a free-elections clause. C discusses state responsibility but omits the key constitutional contrast. D shifts to legislative guidance. The best generalization compares universal voting-right clauses with less consistent free-elections mandates. The answer is A.
